message:
wa-nu4.3.2.5: add `wa accounts` human CLI command

Adds human-readable `wa accounts` command for viewing and managing API
accounts, mirroring the existing robot accounts functionality with rich
table output.

- Add Accounts variant to Commands enum with list/refresh subcommands
- Add AccountListRenderer in output module with table display, pick
  preview, and JSON output support
- Handle refresh subcommand with rate limiting (60s cooldown)
- 7 unit tests for renderer (plain, JSON, empty, pick, threshold)
- 1 integration smoke test (wa accounts --help)
- 14/14 docs smoke tests pass, 2272+ wa-core tests pass, clean clippy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.9","title":"Remote setup + wezterm config generator tests (fixtures, dry-run behavior, logging)","description":"# Task: Remote setup + config generator tests

## Goal
Ensure remote setup and config generation remain safe and deterministic.

## Testing
- Config generator fixture tests:
  - inserts WA block
  - updates WA block
  - does not duplicate

- Remote setup command-building tests:
  - produces the expected ssh/scp invocations in dry-run mode
  - redacts sensitive values in logs
  - emits step boundaries + durations so failures are diagnosable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests do not require real SSH connectivity.
- Logging output includes step boundaries + durations.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4","title":"[EPIC] Diagnostic Bundle + Crash Capture","description":"# [EPIC] Diagnostic Bundle + Crash Capture

## Mission
Make it easy to answer "Is wa healthy?" and "Why did it do that?" without guesswork, including crash-only diagnostics.

## Deliverables
- `wa doctor` checks (environment + config + DB)
- health snapshot plumbing from the watcher
- diagnostic bundle export (sanitized)
- crash bundle capture + listing/triage integration
- performance budget checks in CI

## Deferred
- Incident/reproduce workflows are deferred until demand (see wa-upg.1).

## Testing & observability (non-negotiable)
Diagnostics tooling is only valuable if it is reliable and safe.

- Unit/integration tests must verify:
  - doctor checks against fixtures (no real WezTerm required)
  - diagnostic/crash bundle contents and layout
  - redaction: bundles/logs never leak secrets

- E2E scripts must verify:
  - `wa doctor` behaves correctly in healthy vs broken scenarios
  - crashes produce actionable messages and point to artifacts

## Success Criteria
- `wa doctor`/`wa status` provide actionable health signals (DB connectivity, queue depths, ingest lag, lock state).
- Diagnostic/crash bundles include logs, effective config, and redacted diagnostics for bug reports.
- Performance budgets exist for core hot paths and regressions are visible in CI.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","title":"[Human command] `wa doctor` (environment + config + DB checks)","description":"# Task: Human command wa doctor

## Goal
Provide a deterministic checklist that validates the wa environment.

Doctor is a user trust surface: it must make failures actionable and help users understand where wa stores state.

## Checks (initial)
- Workspace/config resolution
  - print resolved workspace root
  - print resolved DB/log/lock/socket paths
  - verify workspace is writable

- WezTerm
  - wezterm CLI available (wezterm cli list works)
  - wezterm version meets minimum supported
  - scrollback settings are sufficient (warn if too low; best-effort)

- Storage
  - DB is readable/writable
  - schema/migration version matches
  - WAL + foreign_keys configured

- Watcher status (if running)
  - last tick time
  - tailer count
  - backlog sizes

- Feature availability
  - browser (playwright)
  - mcp
  - web/tui/sync (optional)

## Output
- Rich panels when TTY.
- Non-TTY JSON suitable for automation.

## Safety
- Output must never include secrets (even if config contains tokens).
- Redaction must be applied to any potentially sensitive field.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests: `wa-nu4.3.4.8` (fixture-based checks + output stability)
- E2E: `wa-4vx.10.22` (healthy/broken scenarios with verbose artifacts)

## Acceptance Criteria
- On a healthy system, wa doctor exits 0 with a concise summary.
- On a broken system, it exits non-zero and lists actionable fixes.
- Output never includes secrets (even if config contains tokens).

Added --json flag that outputs structured JSON with all diagnostic checks, runtime health, crash history, and circuit breakers. Also fixed pre-existing test issue (verbose: false → verbose: 0 type mismatch). All tests pass: 20 wa tests, 1139+101 wa-core tests.","created_at":"2026-01-29T06:42:38Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.2","title":"Expose watcher health snapshot in CLI (status/health command)","description":"# Task: Expose watcher health snapshot

## Goal
Make the watcher’s health state visible so we can quickly diagnose:
- stuck tailers
- slow polling
- DB backpressure
- pattern match overload

## Metrics (example)
- discovery tick duration
- tailer count + per-domain breakdown
- per-pane last_seen + last_seq
- storage write queue depth
- event bus queue depths
- FTS insert latency

## Deliverables
- `wa status --health` (or `wa health`) prints the snapshot.
- Robot/MCP can access the same snapshot if useful.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - health snapshot JSON is schema-parseable and stable
  - “stuck pane” synthetic scenario is surfaced clearly

- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (stable JSON output in non-TTY mode)

- Diagnostic coverage:
  - `wa doctor` tests (`wa-nu4.3.4.8`) should validate that health signals are consistent with watcher state when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- When a pane stops producing output, health snapshot highlights it clearly.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.3","title":"Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized)

## Goal
Generate a single artifact users can attach to bug reports that contains:
- environment info (OS, wa version)
- config summary (redacted)
- DB health stats (counts, schema version, WAL size)
- last N events + workflow step logs (redacted)
- **active pane reservations + recent reservation conflicts (redacted)**

## Safety requirements
- Never include secrets.
- Apply PolicyEngine redaction rules to any text fields.
- Prefer summaries over raw transcripts.

## Output
- A directory or archive (e.g. `.tar.zst`) written to a user-specified path.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests: `wa-nu4.3.4.7` validates:
  - redaction (no secrets)
  - stable file layout
  - stable metadata fields
  - works while watcher is running

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle can be generated while watcher is running.
- Bundle contents are stable and documented.


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.9.2: Implement config loader: defaults + file + env + CLI overrides; show effective config ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.6: Retention & hygiene: prune old segments, maintenance log, explicit vacuum command ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.2: DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.5: Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.7: Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.4","title":"Perf budgets + regression checks (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS inserts)","description":"# Task: Perf budgets + regression checks

## Goal
Define and enforce basic performance budgets so wa stays fast as features accrete.

## Key hot paths
- Quick reject / prefiltering (if applicable)
- Delta extraction + seq assignment
- Pattern detection over deltas
- DB writes (segments + FTS)

## Deliverables
- Criterion benches for hot paths (some may already exist; extend to cover end-to-end ingest tick).
- Budget thresholds (warn or fail) suitable for CI.

## Testing / CI expectations
- Use coarse budgets to avoid noisy failures.
- CI should detect gross regressions (order-of-magnitude) reliably and upload benchmark artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI can detect a gross regression (e.g., 10× slowdown) reliably.
- Benches are stable (avoid noisy wall-clock assertions; use coarse budgets).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","title":"Metrics endpoint (Prometheus): wa watch --metrics bind + core counters/histograms","description":"# Task: Metrics endpoint (Prometheus)

## Goal
Expose optional Prometheus metrics to support long-running reliability:
- ingest lag
- gaps
- queue depths
- pattern detection latency
- workflow outcomes
- DB write latencies

## Design
- Disabled by default.
- Bind address configurable (default localhost).
- Must not significantly slow down hot path.

## Safety
- Default bind is localhost-only.
- Binding publicly requires an explicit, scary flag (proposed): `--dangerous-bind-any`.
- Metrics must never include transcript contents or secrets.

## Deliverables
- `wa watch --metrics <bind>` and/or config-driven enable.
- A minimal set of stable metric names.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - metric names are stable
  - registry can be instantiated with metrics disabled (zero overhead path)

- Integration tests:
  - start watcher with metrics enabled (bind to localhost ephemeral port)
  - fetch `/metrics` and assert:
    - response is valid Prometheus text format
    - key metrics appear (even if counts are zero)

- Safety tests:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - enabling metrics does not leak secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- When enabled, `/metrics` responds with valid Prometheus text format.
- When disabled, zero overhead on hot path.
","notes":"Review: repo already includes metrics endpoint implementation and CLI flags (files: crates/wa-core/src/metrics.rs, crates/wa/src/main.rs, metrics config). No net code changes needed. fmt OK. cargo check/clippy/test fail due to missing asupersync (fastapi-core path dep), so I could not re-verify via build.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderSnow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:43:39.787589777Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:24:46.944658902Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-metrics","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
